name: Massachusetts SNAP Benefits
description: Massachusetts SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card to help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries. Administered by the Department of Transitional Assistance (DTA).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households
languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, Haitian Creole, Chinese

name: MassHealth (Massachusetts Medicaid)
description: Massachusetts Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage including doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, behavioral health, dental, and vision. One of the most comprehensive state Medicaid programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 300% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 200% FPL
languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, Haitian Creole, Chinese, Vietnamese

name: Massachusetts Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Massachusetts offers up to 30 weeks of benefits, among the longest in the nation.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit is approximately 50% of average weekly wage, up to a maximum

name: MassHousing
description: Massachusetts Housing Finance Agency providing affordable housing programs including mortgage loans, down payment assistance, and rental housing financing. Helps first-time homebuyers and supports affordable rental development.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Massachusetts
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and region; generally targets households below 80% AMI for rental programs

name: Massachusetts Legal Aid - Greater Boston Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in the greater Boston area. Handles cases involving housing, family law, immigration, public benefits, elder law, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of greater Boston with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level
languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, Haitian Creole, Chinese

name: Massachusetts Legal Aid - MetroWest Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income individuals in the MetroWest area covering Framingham, Natick, Marlborough, and surrounding communities. Handles housing, family, benefits, and immigration cases.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of MetroWest Massachusetts with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: MassHire Career Centers
description: Massachusetts' network of career centers providing free employment services including job search assistance, career counseling, resume help, training referrals, and workshops for job seekers.
eligibilitySummary: All Massachusetts job seekers and employers

name: Massachusetts LIHEAP - Fuel Assistance
description: Provides financial assistance to low-income households to help pay winter heating bills. Assistance amounts depend on income, household size, and fuel type. Program runs November through April.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 60% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Massachusetts 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Massachusetts residents to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Massachusetts seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Greater Boston Food Bank
description: The largest hunger relief organization in New England and a Feeding America member food bank. Distributes food to over 600 partner agencies across eastern Massachusetts.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in eastern Massachusetts

name: The Food Bank of Western Massachusetts
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving Franklin, Hampden, Hampshire, and Berkshire counties. Distributes millions of pounds of food through a network of partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in western Massachusetts

name: Massachusetts Department of Veterans' Services
description: Provides services to Massachusetts veterans including benefits counseling, Chapter 115 financial assistance, employment help, housing assistance, and connections to VA services.
eligibilitySummary: Massachusetts veterans and their dependents
incomeLimitNotes: Chapter 115 benefits have income and asset limits

name: Massachusetts Samaritans Crisis Line
description: Provides free, confidential, 24/7 emotional support for people experiencing depression, loneliness, anxiety, or suicidal thoughts. Run by trained volunteers with professional backup.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing emotional distress, depression, or suicidal thoughts
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Massachusetts Child Care Financial Assistance
description: Helps income-eligible families pay for child care through subsidies and vouchers. Families can choose from licensed child care centers, family child care homes, and after-school programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children who need child care for work, school, or training
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of state median income to enter; up to 85% SMI to continue

name: Massachusetts TAFDC (Cash Assistance)
description: Transitional Aid to Families with Dependent Children provides cash assistance and support services to low-income families. Includes employment services and education support.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below state TAFDC limits which vary by family size

name: Massachusetts WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Massachusetts Residential Assistance for Families in Transition (RAFT)
description: Provides short-term financial assistance to families at risk of homelessness. Can help with rent arrears, first/last month rent, security deposits, utility arrears, and moving costs.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children at risk of homelessness due to financial hardship
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 50% of area median income

name: MBTA Reduced Fare Programs
description: The Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority offers reduced fares for seniors, people with disabilities, and low-income youth on all MBTA bus, subway, commuter rail, and ferry services.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 65+, people with disabilities, and low-income youth in the MBTA service area
hours: MBTA service runs daily; customer service Mon-Fri 6:30 AM - 8:00 PM

name: City of Boston Office of Housing Stability
description: Helps Boston residents facing housing instability, eviction, or foreclosure. Provides tenant assistance, mediation, legal referrals, and emergency rental assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Boston residents facing housing instability or eviction
languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese, Haitian Creole, Chinese

name: Worcester Community Action Council
description: Community action agency providing comprehensive services to low-income individuals in central Massachusetts including energy assistance, Head Start, housing, and workforce development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in central Massachusetts
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Massachusetts Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades to reduce energy costs and improve comfort.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 60% of state median income
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Massachusetts Rehabilitation Commission
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Offers job training, education support, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical, mental, or learning disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Massachusetts ConnectorCare
description: Provides subsidized health insurance plans with low premiums and reduced cost-sharing for Massachusetts residents who are not eligible for MassHealth but have low to moderate income.
eligibilitySummary: Massachusetts residents with income between 138-300% FPL who do not have access to employer insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Income between 138% and 300% of the federal poverty level
hours: Monday-Friday, 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM; Saturday 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM

name: Massachusetts Adult Basic Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, numeracy, and workforce readiness at locations across Massachusetts.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 16+ who need basic education, GED, or English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Massachusetts
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Massachusetts Domestic Violence SafeLink Hotline
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals to shelters and services for survivors of domestic violence. Operated by Casa Myrna.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 130+ languages

name: Massachusetts MASSRide Transportation
description: Provides transportation assistance for eligible seniors and people with disabilities through regional transit authorities. Includes paratransit, demand response, and volunteer driver programs.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors, people with disabilities, and those without access to transportation

name: Massachusetts MassAbility
description: Provides services for people with disabilities including community living, brain injury services, independent living centers, and assistive technology. Helps individuals live independently in their communities.
eligibilitySummary: Massachusetts residents with disabilities

name: Springfield Partners for Community Action
description: Community action agency serving the Springfield area providing energy assistance, housing services, youth programs, Head Start, and other anti-poverty programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Springfield area

name: Massachusetts MASSGrant Plus
description: State financial aid program providing grants to Massachusetts residents attending public colleges and universities. Covers tuition and mandatory fees with no repayment required.
eligibilitySummary: Massachusetts residents attending public colleges or universities with financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate financial need as determined by FAFSA
